aspects.name: Evolutionary History
aspects.description: Explores the origins and evolutionary pathways of dinosaurs and other extinct vertebrates.
aspects.purpose: To understand how dinosaurs and extinct vertebrates evolved, including their relationships to modern species.

aspects.name: Anatomy and Physiology
aspects.description: Examines the physical structures and biological functions of extinct vertebrates.
aspects.purpose: To provide insight into how dinosaurs and other extinct species were adapted to their environments and lifestyles.

aspects.name: Fossil Evidence and Paleontology
aspects.description: Studies fossil discoveries and the methods used to interpret extinct life forms.
aspects.purpose: To teach students about fossil analysis techniques and how they reveal information about extinct organisms.

aspects.name: Behavior and Ecology
aspects.description: Investigates the behaviors, habitats, and ecological roles of dinosaurs and extinct vertebrates.
aspects.purpose: To explore the ecosystems these species inhabited and how they interacted with their environments.

aspects.name: Extinction Events
aspects.description: Analyzes the major extinction events that affected dinosaurs and other vertebrates.
aspects.purpose: To understand the causes and consequences of mass extinctions and their impact on biodiversity through time.
